title: Modify secrets pkg
body:
Made a mistake in my earlier patch. I though that if you add an empty string
to an array, the length of the array would still be 0...
Realised this when vendoring the secrets pkg into cri-o.

Signed-off-by: umohnani8 <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body:
I was being an idiot, sorry :(
@rhatdan @mheon PTAL
@umohnani8 I'm confused here - why didn't this work as originally written? Where was the empty string getting into the array?

@umohnani8 But we should never call WithDefaultMountsFile if `--default-mounts-file` is not set. The default for arrays is nil, so the array will remain nil if we don't explicitly set it via WithDefaultMountsFile
To make sure we never set rt.config.DefaultMountsFile to an array with the empty string, we should add a check in the With... function to error out if we get ""

@mheon I agree but can we leave it as a string instead? it would be easier to integrate in buildah and cri-o as I would just have to check for an empty string. And in this case we won't need a check in the `WithDefaultMountsFile` as the zero value of a string is "" anyway.

@umohnani8 We should still add the check in `WithDefaultMountsFile` to make sure people don't accidentally set a bad value.

If using a string instead of []string makes this easier in Buildah and CRI-O, I'm fine with that.
@mheon I am confused, so an empty string ("") is a bad value? If the path doesn't exist the code logs a warning for the user.

@umohnani8 It's fine to use internally, but I like to do validation checking on external APIs. The empty string is not a valid path, so we shouldn't let people explicitly set it as the file path for the mounts file - if they want the default, they shouldn't pass `WithDefaultMountsFile` at all

@mheon but if `DefaultMountsFile == ""` the secrets code bypasses it and uses the defined paths for the mount files anyway. I don't see the need for an extra check in `WithDefaultMountsFile`.
But I can add it in if you think it is for the best. So if `mountsFile == ""`, `WithDefaultMountsFile` should return an error?

@umohnani8 Yeah, return `ErrInvalidArg`. The internal APIs may accept empty string, but the external API users of libpod get shouldn't make assumptions about the way things work inside, so we should validate input we get from users, and discard bad values.

That sounds good to me.  My only thought would be do we have a way to signal to the program not to do any "secret" mounts at all other then creating an empty file and passing it in.  But since this is really just for qe and CI/CD testing, since it is hidden,  that is not too high a burden.
The mounts file should be just a string, not a list of strings.
Tests pass, can we get this in? @mheon @rhatdan

:pushpin: Commit d25b343 has been approved by `mheon`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=mheon d25b34388f51d3446caae476775dd3711d5c4839 -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
